meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Overcome Project Block for Students
content.seo_description: Learn to use AI debugging to solve project paralysis. A beginner-friendly resource for building your first SaaS app using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at a blank IDE. Use AI to identify the smallest viable feature within a complex idea, then leverage Claude Code to write the scaffolding. By debugging AI-generated code, you transition from a passive learner to an active engineer, building your portfolio one resolved error at a time.
content.intro:
You sit down, fingers poised over the keyboard, ready to conquer the world—only to find the cursor blinking in a void of silence. The 'Project Block' isn't a lack of ambition; it's a lack of feedback loops. You are stuck because you are trying to build a monolith before you have mastered the brickwork.

But what if every error message wasn't a wall, but a roadmap? By treating AI as a senior pair programmer, you can bypass the paralyzing fear of 'doing it wrong' and start iterating immediately. This guide turns the chaotic process of debugging into your most powerful engine for skill acquisition.

steps.title: Define the Micro-Scope
steps.description: Narrow down your project to a single, testable feature. This prevents scope creep and keeps your momentum high.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior software architect. I want to build a simple task manager. Break this down into 5 bite-sized, independent modules, starting with the simplest CRUD operation.

steps.title: Generate the Scaffolding
steps.description: Use AI to lay the foundation so you can focus on logic rather than boilerplate syntax.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular directory structure for a React app with a Node.js backend. Include basic error handling patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'generate boilerplate for react node project'

steps.title: Introduce Controlled Bugs
steps.description: Ask the AI to insert a specific logic error so you can practice tracing it manually before using AI to fix it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a snippet of code for a user authentication route, but intentionally include a common security vulnerability. I want to try and spot it first.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'show me the auth route code'

steps.title: Iterative Debugging Loop
steps.description: Use Claude Code to analyze the trace and explain the 'why' behind the failure, not just the 'what'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I have an error in my fetch request. Here is the stack trace. Explain the root cause like I am a junior dev, then provide the fix.

steps.title: Document and Refactor
steps.description: Finalize the module by documenting your learnings to reinforce the neural pathways of the fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the debugging process we just went through. Write a technical README section explaining the common pitfall we solved.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'update README with technical insights from this session'

pro_tips: Treat AI suggestions as drafts, not truth; always verify the logic against official documentation.
Use 'Explain like I'm a Senior' prompts when you want to learn deep architectural patterns.
Keep your context window clean; start a new chat session for every major feature branch to avoid hallucination drift.
Focus on the 'Why' of an error message; if you don't understand the fix, you haven't actually learned.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for every bug you fix to ensure the issue never returns.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your first module, deploy it to a platform like Vercel or Netlify. Seeing your code live on a URL is the ultimate confidence booster.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the methodology to stop waiting for inspiration and start engineering it. Don't let another day pass with a blank IDE. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
